summary:
The facial nerve does not contain special visceral aferent fibers; instead, it conveys general somatic afferents from the external ear. Thus, its sensory pathways do not connect to specific nuclei associated with special visceral afferences such as the nucleus of the tractus solitarius.  

Final Answer: None of these apply correctly based on accurate anatomical principles.
